methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.1B - $8.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Varda Space Industries, a Series C-stage space technology company founded by Will Bruey and Delian Asparouhov, is evaluated against a universe of 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers in Space & Aerospace, including Sierra Space, Skyryse, and SpaceX. The company raised $131M to reach its current $1.5B post-money valuation.

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, implying $2.5B. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies yields a $2.0B median (range: $1.1B–$8.0B), with comparable valuations including Sierra Space at $8.0B (Series C), Apex Space at $2.3B (Series D), and Skyryse at $1.1B (Series C). Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, though the 5-ticker public median trades at 25.8x P/S.

The two applicable methods converge on a valuation range of $1.7B to $2.9B, with a triangulated median of $2.3B. This reflects modest expansion above the current $1.5B valuation. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory and capital efficiency relative to stage-peer performance, particularly given the wide dispersion among private comparables ($1.1B–$8.0B).
